goal: Unify WhatIfInvest asset visuals with app-owned SwiftUI badges instead of first-letter placeholders or official brand logos.
why_now: The current result UI uses a first-letter circle for the selected asset, which feels generic and ticker-centric. A consistent asset badge system can make ETF and stock scenarios more recognizable while avoiding trademark and external asset-management risk.
in_scope: Create a reusable SwiftUI asset badge component
Use circular badges with ticker text and the existing per-asset tint
Apply badges across the scenario picker, result summary, comparison rows, timeline legend, and share card
Keep the current menu-style asset Picker
non_goals: Using official Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Google, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, ETF provider, or other third-party logos
Creating or bundling PNG image assets for this pass
Changing the product terminology from Asset or changing localization copy unless required by implementation
Adding visual patterns, sector illustrations, or shape variants beyond a circular badge
Adding new supported assets beyond the current ETF plus Magnificent 7 set

decision_boundaries.decision: Introduce official or third-party brand logos
decision_boundaries.notes: The approved direction explicitly excludes official logos and brand-owned icons.

decision_boundaries.decision: Change from circular badges to rounded-square or mixed-shape badges
decision_boundaries.notes: The approved design keeps the current circular visual language.

decision_boundaries.decision: Use SwiftUI implementation details that preserve the existing menu Picker
decision_boundaries.notes: Implementation may choose the most reliable SwiftUI row composition as long as the menu Picker remains the selection control.

decision_boundaries.decision: Add a reusable badge component in the shared feature layer
decision_boundaries.notes: A shared component boundary is required to keep the badge consistent across app and share surfaces.

constraints: Follow the existing SwiftUI-first app architecture
Do not custom-skin Apple standard controls beyond adding allowed row content
Keep the design aligned with the Coinbase-inspired light fintech visual system
Use current AssetID symbol, displayName, and tint data where possible
Support English and Korean flows without introducing unlocalized user-facing copy

acceptance_criteria.description: A reusable SwiftUI AssetBadgeView renders circular app-owned badges for every current AssetID.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Code review plus iOS simulator build

acceptance_criteria.description: No official brand logos, trademark-like marks, or PNG logo assets are introduced.
Asset display points in ExploreView and ShareCardView use the shared badge instead of ad hoc dots or first-letter fallback.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Code review and visual simulator pass

acceptance_criteria.description: Ticker text remains readable or gracefully scaled in compact badge sizes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Visual inspection on simulator at compact, standard, and share-card sizes

acceptance_criteria.description: Badges provide accessible labels based on asset symbol and display name without relying on color alone.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Code review and accessibility inspector spot check
